authors: De La Porte C, Van de Kelft E
title: Spinal cord stimulation in failed back surgery syndrome
study_question: Can selected FBSS patients achieve long-term pain control with spinal cord stimulation?
population_assessed: 78 FBSS patients without surgically treatable lesions; 64 received implants.
outcome_measures: Pain relief, medication use, lifestyle or activity changes, continued device use
inclusion_criteria: FBSS, no surgical lesion, psychiatric stability, strong trial-stimulation pain relief
answer_to_study_question: About 54-55% achieved long-term success; medication use and lifestyle also improved.
reasoning: Bibliographic fields strongly agreed. Chose 52(1) over 52(0) by two-model consensus. Used PubMed link from one high-confidence model. Sample was 78 assessed, 64 implanted; kept 78 as study population. Study design favored retrospective cohort by two models despite one case-series label.
field_level_decisions_json: {"volume_issue_pages": "Two models gave 52(1):55-61; one lower-confidence outlier gave issue 0.", "doi": "No model provided a valid DOI; returned null.", "pubmed_hyperlink": "Accepted sole high-confidence PubMed URL.", "study_design": "Majority favored retrospective_cohort_controlled; confidence similar but consensus stronger.", "number_of_participants_in_study": "Used 78 because all patients were assessed; 64 appears implanted subgroup."}
